title: ACT static-deliverable file set
description: Build-time enumeration of the static files a generator MUST emit for a manifest declaring `delivery: "static"`. This schema is informational: it describes the *contract* between PRD-105 and PRD-400 (the generator series), not a wire envelope a consumer fetches. PRD-600's static-profile probe MAY load this shape from a generator's manifest output to predict what to crawl. See PRD-105.

manifest_path.description: Filesystem path (relative to the build output root) of the emitted manifest. Typically equal to well_known_path.

well_known_path.description: URL path the manifest MUST be reachable at. PRD-101 owns the well-known location; PRD-105 restates it for completeness.

index_path.description: URL path of the index file. MUST equal the manifest's `index_url`.

index_ndjson_path.description: URL path of the NDJSON index. REQUIRED iff conformance_level is `"plus"`. Otherwise null.

node_paths.description: URL paths for every node referenced in the index. One entry per index entry; each path is the result of substituting the node id into `node_url_template`.

subtree_paths.description: URL paths for every advertised subtree. REQUIRED at Standard or Plus when subtree availability is claimed for any node. Each path is the result of substituting the subtree-root id into `subtree_url_template`.

search_path.description: URL path of the static search index, if the producer chose to fulfill `search_url_template` via a precomputed JSON. Null when the static profile cannot fulfill query-substitution semantics, in which case `search_url_template` MUST be omitted from the manifest (PRD-107-R10).

conformance_level.description: Mirrors the manifest's `conformance.level`. Used by validators to enforce the right minimum file set.
